tldr: Moving from Plain to Forem is a workflow shift, not a like-for-like migration: Plain handles one-to-one in-app support while Forem powers a public, peer-to-peer community. Plan to run both in parallel during the transition rather than treating Forem as a drop-in replacement.
why_switch: Shifting from private support tickets to public community Q&A
Building a developer-facing community with posts, follows, and reactions
Reducing per-seat support spend by routing repeat questions to a community
Owning the platform end-to-end via open-source self-hosting
what_to_export: Customer list with email and identifying metadata (for Forem invites)
Conversation history from Plain for archival reference
Internal notes and tags applied to conversations
Integration configuration (Slack, email routing, in-app SDK)
Knowledge base articles or canned responses you've built up
FAQ patterns — recurring question themes worth seeding the community with
Customer attributes you want to preserve as Forem profile fields
export_method: Plain exposes a documented support API that you can use to pull conversations, customers, internal notes, and team member data. Use the API to export conversation history as JSON for archival; this is read-only reference data, not something to import wholesale into a community platform. From the export, identify the top recurring question themes — these are the seed posts for your Forem community. Pull your customer list and decide who you want to invite to the community (this is opt-in, not an automatic migration). Document team members and their roles for re-invitation on Forem. Capture in-app SDK initialization config so you can plan the cutover or parallel run. Save canned responses and any knowledge base content as portable Markdown for re-publishing as Forem posts.
import_method: Forem is open-source software you self-host (it powers dev.to). Spin up a Forem instance — this requires real Rails ops work, including a Postgres database, Redis, image storage, and SMTP for invites. Configure your community's branding, tags, and moderation rules in the Forem admin. Seed the community with 10-20 posts derived from the recurring themes in your Plain conversation history; these give early visitors something to engage with. Invite your team as moderators and a curated set of customers as initial members. Wire up SSO if you want to use your existing identity provider. Decide on the relationship with Plain: most teams keep Plain running for in-app one-to-one support and use Forem for public Q&A — they complement each other rather than replace each other. If you do retire Plain entirely, plan for the customer expectation shift from private ticket to public post.

concept_mapping.note: Private one-to-one becomes public many-to-many — workflow shift, not a data mapping

gotchas: Plain and Forem solve different problems. Treat this as a workflow change, not a data migration; most teams run both in parallel rather than swap one for the other.
Forem is self-hosted Rails software. Budget real ops time for setup, upgrades, backups, and monitoring before assuming the migration is cheap.
Private support history cannot become public community posts without explicit customer consent. Use Plain conversations to identify themes, not to bulk-import content.
Customers expect different response SLAs in a public community vs a private support queue. Communicate the change before redirecting traffic.
Forem theming and customization beyond the basics requires code changes; budget design time if you need a branded experience.
Identity and SSO need to be set up fresh on Forem; do not assume Plain identities map over.

email.subject: Heads up: we're launching a community alongside support
email.body:
Hi team,

We're standing up a Forem-based community over the next 14 days. This is not a replacement for Plain — Plain stays for in-app one-to-one support, and Forem will host public Q&A and discussion. The goal is to let recurring questions get answered once, publicly, instead of repeatedly in tickets.

Action items: support team will get moderator invites this week. Please help seed the first posts from common ticket themes. Engineering is handling the Forem deploy and SSO wiring.

Reply with questions.

Thanks,
Your customer
